pattern: Count permutations of 0..n satisfying a D/I pattern by tracking the relative rank of the last placed element across a shrinking pool, updated with a running prefix or suffix sum.
hints: Instead of tracking absolute values, track the rank of each placed element within the pool of values still available — this rank runs from 0 to (remaining pool size - 1) and resets cleanly at each step.

For an 'I' step, the new element's rank in the fresh pool must map to an old-pool rank strictly greater than the previous rank, which simplifies to a prefix sum over old dp values up to and including the new rank index.
Use prefix sums (for 'I') and suffix sums (for 'D') to compute each dp row in O(n) time, giving an overall O(n^2) algorithm.

edge_inputs.description: single character D — only one decreasing permutation of [0,1]
single character I — only one increasing permutation of [0,1]
two characters II — only strictly increasing [0,1,2]
two characters DD — only strictly decreasing [2,1,0]
DID from the statement — a common wrong answer is 3 (off by prefix-sum direction)
all I then all D — tests boundary between rising and falling halves
longer alternating DIDI — answer grows rapidly, tests accumulation under modulo

stress.description: random D/I string of length up to 200 — exercises the full n range with modular arithmetic
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = rng.randint(1, size)
    s = ''.join(rng.choice(['D', 'I']) for _ in range(n))
    return [s]

solutions.python:
class Solution:
    def numPermsDISequence(self, s: str) -> int:
        MOD = 10**9 + 7
        n = len(s)
        # dp[j] = number of valid placements of the first i+1 elements
        # where the i-th element has rank j in the remaining pool of size (n+1-i)
        dp = [1] * (n + 1)
        for i in range(1, n + 1):
            new_dp = [0] * (n + 1 - i)
            if s[i - 1] == 'I':
                # new element rank j must map to old-pool rank > prev rank k,
                # which holds exactly when k <= j — accumulate as prefix sum
                prefix = 0
                for j in range(n + 1 - i):
                    prefix = (prefix + dp[j]) % MOD
                    new_dp[j] = prefix
            else:  # 'D'
                # new element rank j must map to old-pool rank < prev rank k,
                # which holds exactly when k > j — accumulate as suffix sum
                suffix = 0
                for j in range(n - i, -1, -1):
                    suffix = (suffix + dp[j + 1]) % MOD
                    new_dp[j] = suffix
            dp = new_dp
        return sum(dp) % MOD
